stories.title: Cameron Young Goes Wire-to-Wire at Cadillac Championship — Second Win of 2026 at Doral

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
CAMERON YOUNG dominates Doral. Wire-to-wire. -19. Six shots clear of Scottie Scheffler.

Two wins in 2026 and the season is barely four months old. Young is playing the best golf of his career.

#GolfFanrecap #PGATour #CadillacChampionship

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Final leaderboard — 2026 Cadillac Championship:

1. Cameron Young -19 ✅
2. Scottie Scheffler -13
T3. Ben Griffin -12
T4. Si Woo Kim / Sepp Straka / Adam Scott -11

Young earns $3.6M. The Blue Monster was tamed.

#GolfFanrecap #PGATour #CadillacChampionship

facebook.long_form:
Cameron Young walked off the 18th green at Trump National Doral on Sunday with a six-shot victory and $3.6 million in his pocket — and the look of a player who knows he has something special going in 2026.

Young dominated the Cadillac Championship from the opening tee shot, building leads of one, five, and six strokes after each of the first three rounds in a wire-to-wire performance that was never in serious doubt. His final score of 19-under 269 left Scottie Scheffler and the rest of the Blue Monster field six shots back. Scheffler — the world No. 1 and defending Masters champion — had no answer for the relentless scoring that Young produced across four days at Doral.

It is Young's second win of the 2026 season, a further statement from a player who had long been talked about as a future winner on the PGA Tour and is now delivering on that promise in emphatic fashion. Ben Griffin finished alone in third at 12-under, while Si Woo Kim, Sepp Straka, and Adam Scott tied for fourth at 11-under in a quality field.

The final round tee times were moved up due to inclement weather in South Florida — but the conditions couldn't derail Young's march to the title. He was simply the best player in the field all week.
facebook.image_caption: Wire-to-wire. 19-under. Six shots clear. Cameron Young owns the 2026 Cadillac Championship — his second win of the season and a statement victory at Trump National Doral.

article.headline: Wire-to-Wire and Six Clear: Cameron Young Dominates the Cadillac Championship for Second Win of 2026

stories.title: Nelly Korda Wins Riviera Maya Open for Third LPGA Title of 2026 — Historic Consistency

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
NELLY KORDA wins again. Third LPGA title of 2026.

17-under in Mexico. Four-shot victory. Six tournaments in 2026 — zero finishes outside the top 2.

Someone has to stop her. Nobody has figured out how.

#GolfFanrecap #LPGA #RivieraMayaOpen

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda's 2026 season: 3 wins, 6 starts, 0 finishes below No. 2.

The Riviera Maya Open was her most dominant performance yet. And the LPGA Tour resumes next week in New Jersey.

She's not slowing down. #GolfFanrecap #LPGA #NellyKorda

facebook.long_form:
When you haven't finished outside the top two in any of your six starts this season, the question stops being whether you'll win — it becomes how many titles you'll have by the end of the year.

Nelly Korda added a third to her 2026 total on Sunday at the Riviera Maya Open at Mayakoba in Playa del Carmen, Mexico, closing out a four-shot victory at the El Camaleon Golf Course. Her final score of 17-under left Arpichaya Yubol four strokes behind in second, with China's Yu Liu third and Patty Tavatanakit fourth.

The numbers Korda is putting up in 2026 are extraordinary. Three wins. Six starts. Not once has she finished lower than second. The consistency — particularly on a tour that features players from more than 60 countries competing at the highest level — is not just impressive. According to Sky Sports, it ties her with Annika Sorenstam as the only woman to achieve that level of sustained excellence across the opening stretch of a season.

The LPGA Tour continues next week with the Mizuho Americas Open in New Jersey. Korda will be the story wherever she tees it up next.
facebook.image_caption: 3 wins. 6 starts. 0 finishes outside the top 2. Nelly Korda is the story of the 2026 LPGA Tour — and she just claimed the Riviera Maya Open by four shots. Dominant.

article.headline: Korda Claims Third LPGA Win of 2026 at Riviera Maya Open — Historic Consistency Continues

stories.title: PGA Championship Preview — Aronimink Golf Club, May 14-17; Scheffler Favored

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
The 108th PGA Championship is 10 days away.

📍 Aronimink Golf Club — Newtown Square, PA
📅 May 14-17

Scheffler defending. McIlroy fresh off defending the Masters. DeChambeau lurking.

This major is going to be SPECIAL. #GolfFanrecap #PGAChampionship

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Aronimink Golf Club hasn't hosted the PGA Championship since 1962.

64 years later, it gets another chance — and Scottie Scheffler arrives as defending champion and No. 1 in the world.

May 14-17. Circle it. #GolfFanrecap #PGAChampionship #Aronimink

facebook.long_form:
The second major of the golf year is arriving quickly — and the 108th PGA Championship has the kind of storyline package that should make it unmissable viewing.

The championship runs May 14-17 at Aronimink Golf Club in Newtown Square, Pennsylvania, a suburb of Philadelphia. It will be only the second time Aronimink has hosted the PGA Championship — the first was 1962, more than 60 years ago. The course rewards precision over power, featuring rolling fairways, strategic bunkering, and green complexes that test every aspect of a player's game.

Scottie Scheffler arrives as the defending champion, having won last year's PGA Championship by five strokes. He enters as the favorite at roughly +350 and has shown no signs of slowing down — even a second-place finish this week at Doral demonstrates the world No. 1's consistency. Rory McIlroy is second in the odds at around +650, and his recent form makes that feel conservative. McIlroy became the fourth player in Masters history to defend his title earlier this spring, and he enters Aronimink with the kind of confidence that comes from winning majors in back-to-back years.

Bryson DeChambeau (+1000) and Jon Rahm (+1400) round out the early favorites. But a course that rewards precision over distance could bring other players into the conversation.
facebook.image_caption: 108 years of PGA Championship history leads to this: Aronimink Golf Club, May 14-17. Scheffler defending. McIlroy dangerous. The second major of 2026 is almost here.

article.headline: PGA Championship 2026: Aronimink Awaits, Scheffler Defends, McIlroy Hunts Another Major

stories.title: Truist Championship Preview — McIlroy Seeks Record 5th Title at Quail Hollow Starting Thursday

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Rory McIlroy is going to Quail Hollow — and he's chasing something nobody else has.

A record FIFTH Truist Championship title. He's won there in 2010, 2015, 2021 and 2024.

First round Thursday. $20 million on the line. #GolfFanrecap #PGATour #TruistChampionship

facebook.long_form:
Before the PGA Championship arrives at Aronimink, Rory McIlroy has one more thing to take care of: a fifth title at Quail Hollow Club.

McIlroy is committed to play in this week's Truist Championship at Quail Hollow in Charlotte, North Carolina — a Signature Event that tees off Thursday and runs through Sunday, May 10. He has won at Quail Hollow four times already, in 2010, 2015, 2021, and 2024, giving him a record at the venue that is unmatched in the event's history. A fifth would be unprecedented territory.

The field surrounding him is strong. Cameron Young, fresh off his Cadillac Championship victory at Doral, is in the field. Past Truist champions Marc Homa, Jason Day, Brian Harman, Rickie Fowler, and Lucas Glover are also entered. With $20 million and 700 FedExCup points on the line, this is one of the most lucrative events outside of the majors on the schedule.

For McIlroy, a win would also serve as perfect preparation ahead of the PGA Championship the following week. There is no better way to carry momentum into a major than coming off a victory at a familiar venue.
facebook.image_caption: Can Rory McIlroy win a FIFTH time at Quail Hollow? The Truist Championship starts Thursday — and the four-time champion is in the field and hunting more history.

article.headline: McIlroy Returns to Quail Hollow Seeking an Unprecedented Fifth Truist Championship Title

stories.title: LIV Golf Washington DC Preview — Trump National Hosts May 7-10

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
LIV Golf heads to Trump National DC this week — May 7-10.

Jon Rahm is red-hot after winning Mexico City at -21. Can he make it back-to-back?

The LIV Tour heats up as summer approaches. #GolfFanrecap #LIVGolf

facebook.long_form:
LIV Golf takes its show to the Washington DC area this week, with the tour's latest event set for Trump National Golf Club in Sterling, Virginia, running May 7-10.

The event comes with significant momentum from the tour's most recent stop. Jon Rahm won the LIV Golf Mexico City event in dominant fashion at 21-under par, separating himself by six strokes in a commanding display that added to his status as one of the consistent performers on the alternative circuit. Rahm has been one of LIV Golf's most successful players since joining, and the Washington DC event will test whether he can build on that Mexico City form.

LIV Golf's Washington-area event has historically drawn strong crowds and produced competitive golf on the Nicklaus-designed layout. The format — 54 holes of individual and team play — continues to be one of the distinguishing features of the LIV product compared to the traditional PGA Tour.

With the PGA Championship arriving the following week and multiple LIV alumni competing in major championships, the DC event will generate added attention from a golf world that is closely watching the status of every top player heading into Aronimink.
facebook.image_caption: LIV Golf heads to Trump National DC this week, May 7-10. Jon Rahm arrives red-hot after winning in Mexico City at -21. The summer golf calendar is heating up.

article.headline: LIV Golf Comes to Washington DC: Can Jon Rahm Win Back-to-Back at Trump National?
